commit.message:
masternode: Direct TCP connection is authoritative for is_active, gossip is secondary

cleanup_stale_reports was unconditionally overwriting is_active with a
gossip-report count even when the node had a live TCP connection. This caused
OnChain-registered masternodes to flip to inactive every 60s if they had fewer
than min_reports (2-3) gossip witnesses — which happens with any node that has
only 1-2 connections (common in small testnets and pyramid leaf nodes).

Fix: snapshot connected peers at the start of each cleanup cycle and skip the
gossip-based flip for any masternode that is directly connected. Gossip still
controls is_active for nodes we have no direct connection to (the normal
Sybil-resistance case). Pass peer_registry into start_report_cleanup and
cleanup_stale_reports for this check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: Add noclone to DSL_NOINLINE, restore blend_over noinline

Add __attribute__((noclone)) alongside noinline in the DSL_NOINLINE
macro.  GCC 14 was creating constprop clones of noise2, noise3, and
blend_over despite noinline, causing register pressure and I-cache
thrash on the ESP32's 32KB I-cache.

Also restores DSL_NOINLINE on dsl_blend_over() (was changed to inline
in 54ce7b9), which prevented the macro from taking effect.

forest-wind results on hardware:
  Before: 89.9 ms display, 10.9 FPS
  After:  34.2 ms display, 27.9 FPS (2.6× faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: Add comprehensive unit tests and quality checks

Implement comprehensive testing and code quality configuration:

Test Framework:
- JUnit5 (Jupiter) for modern testing capabilities
- Mockito for mocking and interaction verification
- 40 unit tests across 5 test classes
- ~93% average code coverage

Test Classes:
- CartServiceTest: 13 tests for CRUD operations, events, metrics
- CartControllerTest: 11 tests for REST endpoints, validation, errors
- CartEventPublisherTest: 8 tests for Kafka event publishing
- DailyCartStatsSchedulerTest: 3 tests for scheduled job logic
- HealthIndicatorsTest: 5 tests for health check implementations

Code Quality Tools:
- Checkstyle plugin: Google Java Style compliance
- PMD plugin: Static code analysis and best practices
- JaCoCo plugin: Code coverage reporting
- Surefire plugin: Test execution and reporting

Configuration:
- Updated pom.xml with testing dependencies
- Added maven plugins for quality gates
- Test includes: Service, Controller, Events, Scheduler, Health
- Mocking: Spring's MockMvc, Mockito annotations, ArgumentCaptor

Test Coverage:
- CartService: ~95% coverage
- CartController: ~90% coverage
- Event publishing: ~95% coverage
- Scheduler: ~90% coverage
- Health indicators: ~95% coverage

Quality Metrics:
- All tests use proper naming and display names
- Comprehensive error scenario testing
- Input validation verification
- Mock interaction verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix MCP server binding to 0.0.0.0 for container deployments

FastMCP SSE transport defaults to 127.0.0.1 which is inaccessible
from outside the container. Explicitly bind to 0.0.0.0 so ACI/Docker
can route external traffic to the server.

ci: add fuzz corpus sanitizer scan workflow

3-job workflow that cross-repo checks out xsscx/fuzz corpus:
1. build-tools: Build iccanalyzer-lite + IccToXml/IccFromXml with ASAN+UBSAN
2. scan-icc-profiles: Run 95 ICC profiles through analyzer + IccToXml
3. scan-icc-xml: Run 116 XML files through IccFromXml + round-trip validation

Artifacts: per-file ASAN/UBSAN reports, coverage data, markdown summaries
Triggers: push to iccanalyzer-lite/** or cfl/patches/**, workflow_dispatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add integration tests for protocol stack

Implements end-to-end integration tests covering the protocol → rasterizer
→ display pipeline. This addresses audit finding AUDIT-2026-03-07-v3.md
'No integration tests for protocol stack'.

Changes:
- Created internal/integration package with comprehensive integration tests
- TestWaylandClientStack: Validates end-to-end Wayland client with mock
  compositor, testing wl_display.sync request/response cycle
- TestProtocolRasterDisplayPipeline: Verifies wire protocol encoding,
  rasterizer buffer creation, and display preparation
- TestX11ProtocolIntegration: Validates X11 CreateWindow request encoding
- BenchmarkWireEncoding: Performance benchmark for wire protocol encoding

Tests validate component integration without requiring a running compositor
or X server, catching integration bugs that unit tests miss (event sequencing,
buffer lifecycle, fd leakage).

All tests pass (20 packages). Zero regressions in complexity metrics.

Resolves: Audit finding - No integration tests for protocol stack

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace uint.Parse with TryParse on user input in API handlers (#1212)

Replace all uint.Parse calls on user-supplied route parameters, query
strings, and cookie values in the Host project with uint.TryParse and
proper 400 Bad Request error responses. This prevents unhandled
FormatException/OverflowException when clients send non-numeric IDs.

Closes #1181

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit: Wisdom Translator tkinter app

Five-panel translation tool: English → Spanish (Spain), French (France),
Hindi, and Chinese (Simplified) Pinyin. Features:
- 2×2 grid layout for translation panels
- Per-panel scrollable text boxes with Copy buttons
- Auto-translate with debounce (900 ms) + manual Translate button
- 1000-character limit with live counter
- macOS Aqua theme with system colour tokens (Light/Dark mode)
- macOS menu-bar app name override via ObjC runtime (ctypes)
- ~/bin/wisdom-translator zsh launcher

commit.message:
feat: add mock Kubernetes server to Dev Container (#1568)

- [x] Explore repository structure and CI validation workflow
- [x] Install `kind` and `kubectl` in `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` with
renovate-tracked versions
- [x] Add checksum verification for `kind` and `kubectl` downloads
(consistent `awk '{print $1}'` pattern for both)
- [x] Create `.devcontainer/setup-mock-k8s.sh` — idempotent script that
creates the `mock` kind cluster and installs GKE CRDs
- [x] Fix POSIX compatibility: replace `&>` with `>/dev/null 2>&1` in
the `sh -c` wait loop
- [x] Add `kind export kubeconfig` before `kubectl apply` to guarantee
correct cluster context whether cluster is new or pre-existing
- [x] Update `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` to run setup script via
`postCreateCommand`
- [x] Add documentation `docs/dev-container-mock-k8s.md` with
instructions for using the mock server

> - [ ] GKE CRDs from `k8s/mock/crds.yaml` are automatically installed
> - [ ] Developers can run `kubectl apply --dry-run=server` for manifest
validation locally
> - [ ] Documentation is updated with instructions for using the mock
server</issue_description>

commit.message:
fix: set productVersion in wails.json during CI build

Homebrew cask upgrades require the app bundle's Info.plist version
(CFBundleShortVersionString) to match the cask version. Without this,
the hardcoded productVersion in wails.json causes brew upgrade --cask
to fail because the installed app always reports the old version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: accept 'copilot' or 'hey copilot', improved fuzzy matching

Wake phrase matching now:
- Accepts just 'copilot' (or any phrase without leading fillers like 'hey')
  as well as the full configured phrase, so both 'Copilot' and 'Hey Copilot'
  trigger activation.
- Uses 40% edit-distance tolerance per word (max(1, round(len*0.4))):
  'hey'(3)→1 edit, 'copilot'(7)→3 edits, covering mishearings like 'coballot'.
- Guards against empty normalized text causing false positives.
- Only emits 'heard' event when listener is still active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

log: session 11 — Git workflow, CI/CD pipeline, branch protection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Pipeline] Extract compliance decisions lookup into shared ComplianceQueries helper (#408)

Closes #406

## Summary

Eliminates duplicate latest-decision-per-scan query logic that existed
independently in both `ComplianceEndpoints.cs` and
`Compliance.cshtml.cs`.

## Changes

- **`PRDtoProd/Data/ComplianceQueries.cs`** _(new)_ — static
`GetLatestDecisionsAsync(TicketDbContext)` helper that encapsulates the
decisions query, in-memory grouping, and `OrderByDescending`
tie-breaking
- **`ComplianceEndpoints.GetAllScans`** — replaced 11-line inline block
with a single `await ComplianceQueries.GetLatestDecisionsAsync(db)` call
- **`Compliance.cshtml.cs OnGetAsync`** — replaced 9-line inline block
with the same shared call; also fixes a latent null-safety bug (page
model was using `.First()` which throws if the group is somehow empty;
now uses `.FirstOrDefault()` consistently)

## PRD Fidelity

No PRD traceability section — this is a code quality / bug issue
(`#406`) identified by the Duplicate Code Detector workflow, not a
PRD-decomposed feature. The fix is purely internal; no API contracts or
page behaviour are changed.

## Test Results

```
Build succeeded. 0 Warning(s), 0 Error(s).
Passed! Failed: 0, Passed: 165, Skipped: 0, Total: 165
```

commit.message:
feat: consume batch-evaluated task outcomes in world model and trust scoring (Issue 4)

- Add WorldModelUpdater.updateFromBatchOutcomes() that queries
  task_run_outcomes for batch-evaluated results, computes aggregate
  metrics (quality score, first-time accept rate, revision rate,
  blocker rate, abort rate, efficiency), and updates the agent's
  world model strengths/weaknesses/failure_patterns accordingly.

- Wire batch outcome evaluation into batchOutcomeEvaluator.ts: after
  scoring outcomes, group by agent_role and call updateFromBatchOutcomes()
  plus TrustScorer.applyBatchOutcomeDelta() for each agent.

- Add TrustScorer.applyBatchOutcomeDelta() with task_outcome_quality
  signal source (weight 1.0): positive signal for scores >= 4.0,
  negative for <= 2.0, neutral zone between 2.0-4.0.

- Reduce self-assessment weight in baseAgentRunner.ts: task-tier agents
  use 0.3x multiplier on self-scores (batch outcomes are now primary
  quality signal), orchestrators keep 1.0x.

- Add batch outcome metrics section to WorldModel.tsx dashboard showing
  avg quality, first-time accept rate, efficiency, and evaluation count.
  Filter batch_ keys from radar chart data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace legacy I2S with async DAC continuous DMA driver

Migrate fw_audio_output.c from the deprecated legacy I2S DAC path to
ESP-IDF's DAC continuous driver using asynchronous mode. The previous
synchronous/cyclic approach suffered from DMA descriptor pool corruption
during cyclic-to-acyclic transitions, causing ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT on every
write after the first.

Async mode avoids this entirely:
- DMA runs in a permanent ring of 8×256-byte descriptors
- ISR on_convert_done callback fires per descriptor completion
- A fill task on core 0 reads from a 4096-byte lock-free SPSC ring buffer
- Render loop pushes audio into the ring (non-blocking, overflow drops oldest)
- When ring is empty, fill task outputs midscale silence (0x80)

Results verified on hardware:
- A440 test tone: 40 FPS, 1.9ms display + 0.8ms audio
- forest-wind: 10.9 FPS, 89.9ms display + 1.7ms audio (display-bound)
- Clean boot with zero errors, clean shader transitions
- Zero compiler warnings in fullclean build

Also removes the -Wno-cpp/-Wno-deprecated-declarations workaround from
CMakeLists.txt since the legacy driver/i2s.h include is gone.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial plan (#6)

commit.message:
feat(cart-service): Comprehensive cart service enhancement

Implement complete cart service with enterprise features:

Features:
- Full CRUD operations for carts and items
- MongoDB persistence with audit fields (createdAt, updatedAt)
- Kafka event streaming with structured events
- Spring Security with JWKS token validation
- Role-based access control (USER, ADMIN)
- Redis caching with 30-minute TTL and auto-invalidation
- Prometheus metrics and custom business metrics
- OpenTelemetry distributed tracing support
- Health check indicators (MongoDB, Redis, Kafka)
- Daily scheduled job for cart statistics aggregation
- EKS blue-green deployment strategy with K8s manifests

New Components:
- CartService: Complete business logic with circuit breaker & rate limiting
- CartController: Full REST API with validation & error handling
- CartEventPublisher: Kafka producer with retry logic
- DailyCartStatsScheduler: Midnight cron job for stats aggregation
- Custom Health Indicators: MongoDB, Redis, Kafka connectivity checks
- Configuration Classes: Security, Kafka, Cache, Metrics, Scheduler, MongoDB

Infrastructure:
- Updated docker-compose: Added Redis, Kafka (Zookeeper), health checks
- Kubernetes manifests: Blue/Green deployments, Service, ConfigMap, Secret
- application-docker.yml: Docker-specific configuration
- Postman collection: Complete API testing suite

Updated Dependencies:
- Spring Security OAuth2 + JWKS
- Spring Cloud Stream (Kafka)
- Spring Data Redis + Jedis
- Micrometer + OpenTelemetry
- Resilience4j
- Jackson JSON

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Foehammer82 <20054058+Foehammer82@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add document visibility and file path management

- Introduced `is_public` and `file_path` columns to the documents table for better document visibility control.
- Updated the API to allow GMs and admins to manage document visibility, while regular members can only access public documents.
- Enhanced document upload functionality to include visibility settings.
- Implemented document viewer page to display and download documents based on visibility.
- Updated frontend components to reflect changes in document management and visibility.

* refactor: Remove unused imports and clean up code in CampaignDocumentsTab
